hero.eyebrow: Why Hua Hin · Thailand's royal resort
hero.headline: Thailand's royal address.
hero.subtext: For over a century, Thailand's kings, aristocrats, and discerning international buyers have chosen Hua Hin. Not for parties — for permanence.

royal.label: Since the 1920s
royal.headline: Built for royalty. Chosen by the elite.
royal.body: In 1929, King Rama VII commissioned Klai Kangwon Palace — Far from Worries — as the royal summer residence on Hua Hin's shore. It was not a tourist invention. It was a deliberate royal choice: sea breeze, distance from Bangkok politics, and a setting fit for a court.
royal.body2: The royal presence shaped everything — infrastructure, security, cleanliness, atmosphere. Hua Hin has never become a party town. The palace is still actively used by the royal family today.
royal.body3: King Bhumibol Adulyadej (Rama IX) spent decades here. The palace complex and the town grew together — railways, roads, hotels and security infrastructure arrived because the monarchy was present. That legacy still defines the place.

golf.label: Golf
golf.headline: Southeast Asia's golf capital.
golf.body: 10+ championship courses within 30 minutes, including Black Mountain Golf Club and Royal Hua Hin — Thailand's oldest golf course, established 1924 by King Rama VI. Green fees from 1,500–3,500 THB. Year-round play.

location.label: Location
location.headline: 200 km from Bangkok. A world away.
location.body: 2.5 hours by car, direct train from Hua Lamphong. Hua Hin airport with regional connections. Close enough to Bangkok for business. Far enough to forget it.
location.carBody: 2.5–3 hours via Rama II highway. Door-to-door from Bangkok — the most flexible option for families and luggage.
location.trainBody: 3.5–4 hours on the scenic Gulf of Thailand route from Hua Lamphong or Bangkok Thonburi. Sleepers and 1st class A/C available.
location.busBody: About 3.5 hours from Bangkok Southern Bus Terminal — from ฿150. Simple and frequent.
location.airBody: 25 minutes from Hua Hin Airport (HHQ) for regional flights, or fly into Bangkok (Suvarnabhumi ≈ 2.5 hours by road) and continue south.
location.glanceAir: Air — HHQ 25 min from plot · BKK / DMK + drive

airport.label: Hua Hin Airport
airport.headline: Hua Hin Airport — closer than you think.
airport.body: IATA code HHQ. 25 minutes from the plot. Runway widened to 45 meters in 2026 — now Boeing 737 and Airbus A320 capable. International certification targeted for 2026. Direct connections to Kuala Lumpur via AirAsia. Regional network expanding.

train.label: Bangkok by train
train.headline: Bangkok to Hua Hin — 3.5 hours by train.
train.body: Direct train from Bangkok Hua Lamphong station. 3.5–4 hours, scenic Gulf of Thailand route. Multiple departures daily. 1st class air-conditioned carriages available from ฿200–800.
train.body2: Hua Hin Railway Station — built in 1926 in Victorian style, one of the most beautiful stations in Thailand. The Royal Pavilion (Phra Mongkut Klao) was built for the royal family's arrivals.
train.body3: Prefer the bus? Bangkok to Hua Hin takes about 3.5 hours from the Southern Bus Terminal, from ฿150.

quiet.label: Why not Phuket
quiet.headline: Quiet by design.
quiet.intro: Phuket has parties. Pattaya has nightlife. Hua Hin has golf, seafood, jazz bars, and a beach that was good enough for three generations of Thai kings.
quiet.body: Phuket has transformed into a mass tourism destination — traffic, overdevelopment, noise, and a nominee land crisis. Hua Hin remained deliberate. The royal presence kept it that way. The expat community here is families, retirees, and long-term residents — people who chose quality over chaos.
quiet.phuket5: Heavy nominee / company land risk in tourist zones
quiet.huaHin1: Royal town — calm by design since the 1920s
quiet.huaHin2: Golf, seafood, jazz — not full-moon parties
quiet.huaHin3: Families and retirees, not party weekenders
quiet.huaHin4: Genuine expat community with deep roots
quiet.huaHin5: Royal presence shaped security and infrastructure
quiet.nomineeNote: Tourist hotspots also carry higher enforcement attention on nominee land structures in 2026.

expats.label: The community
expats.headline: 71 nationalities. One community.
expats.body: Over 5,000 expats call Hua Hin home, representing 71 nationalities. Scandinavians have been here since the 1990s — drawn by the golf, the climate, and the calm. Europeans, Australians, Americans, and Thais from Bangkok share a genuinely international lifestyle.
expats.body2: Sunday markets, live jazz every night, organic cafes, international schools, Bangkok Hospital 15 minutes away. It is not a retirement ghetto — it is a life.

investment.label: Investment
investment.headline: Why values are rising.
investment.body: Hua Hin's town plan is expanding from 87 to 327 km² — Thap Tai is included. The airport is being upgraded to international standard. Pool villas in the Soi 112 corridor sell for ฿15M–25M. This is why Botanica, La Felice and Mali Prestige all chose this corridor.
investment.point1Body: Hua Hin's municipal plan is expanding from 87 to 327 km² — Thap Tai and the Soi 112 corridor are inside the growth zone.
investment.point2Body: Hua Hin Airport expansion toward international capability (2026) shortens the journey for European and regional buyers.
investment.point3Body: Finished pool villas in this corridor trade around ฿15M–25M — land values trend up as infrastructure and prestige projects land.
investment.point4Body: This is why Botanica, La Felice and Mali Prestige chose Soi 112 — the corridor already carries luxury brand gravity.

foreignBuyers.heroAlt: Hua Hin Chanote land for foreign buyers
foreignBuyers.heroTitle: Buying land in Thailand as a foreigner
foreignBuyers.heroSubtitle: Clear rules. Legal options. What to avoid — so you buy safely in 2026.
foreignBuyers.truthBody: The Land Code reserves freehold land for Thai nationals. That does not block your villa — it means you use a structure Thai law recognises. Most buyers choose a registered 30-year lease on Chanote title.
foreignBuyers.legal1Body: Registered at the Land Office against Chanote. Exclusive use for 30 years, typically renewable. Cleanest path for villa land.
foreignBuyers.legal2Body: Own a condo unit in your name within the foreign quota. Does not apply to land or houses.
foreignBuyers.legal3Body: Long-term stay with expanded rights. Pairs well with a registered lease — does not create freehold land ownership.
foreignBuyers.step1Body: Confirm title at the Land Office or via data.hua-hin-land.com before any deposit.
foreignBuyers.step2Body: Hire an independent Thai property lawyer — not only the seller's counsel.
foreignBuyers.step3Body: Duration, renewals, premium, building ownership and exit rights — in writing.
foreignBuyers.step4Body: Sign and register the lease against the Chanote. Unregistered leases are a red flag.
foreignBuyers.step5Body: Land Office fees, stamp duty, then build under proper permits.
foreignBuyers.flag1: Nominee structures — Thai name holding land "for" a foreigner
foreignBuyers.flag2: Unregistered leases — private contracts only, not at the Land Office
foreignBuyers.flag3: Sor Kor 1 or weak permits sold as "title"
foreignBuyers.ctaTitle: Questions about buying this Chanote plot?
foreignBuyers.ctaSubtitle: Clean Thai seller · No nominee structure · Registered lease path

faqPage.heroSubtitle: Ownership, the plot, process and Hua Hin — straight answers.
faqPage.cat1q1: Can a foreigner buy this land?
faqPage.cat1a1: Foreigners cannot own freehold land in Thailand. The legal path is typically a 30-year lease registered at the Land Office against the Chanote title — often renewable. Condo freehold (49% quota) is a separate option for apartments only. See our foreign buyers guide for the full picture.
faqPage.cat1q2: What is a Chanote title deed?
faqPage.cat1a2: Chanote (NS.4J) is Thailand's strongest land title: GPS-surveyed boundaries, full freehold ownership registered at the Land Department. It can be sold, mortgaged, leased or subdivided. This plot has Chanote NS.4J.
faqPage.cat1a3: Yes. Partial sale is available from 1 rai. Pricing per rai differs from the full-plot rate — ask for current terms. Subdivision is feasible because the title is Chanote.
faqPage.cat1q4: What are the total costs to buy?
faqPage.cat1a4: Land price is from ฿2,300,000/rai (full plot ≈ ฿10,350,000). Budget separately for lawyer fees (often ฿20,000–50,000), Land Office registration fees, and lease registration if you are a foreign buyer. Transfer taxes/fees depend on structure — your lawyer will quote exact figures.
faqPage.cat1q5: How do I verify the title deed?
faqPage.cat1a5: Check official records via data.hua-hin-land.com and confirm at the provincial Land Office with your lawyer before paying any deposit. Never rely on photocopies alone.
faqPage.cat2q1: How far is it from Hua Hin beach?
faqPage.cat2a1: About 12 minutes by car to Hua Hin Beach. Bluport Mall is ~10 minutes; Bangkok Hospital Hua Hin ~15 minutes; Majestic Creek Golf ~5 minutes.
faqPage.cat2q2: Can I build a villa on this land?
faqPage.cat2a2: Yes. The plot is completely flat and build-ready with water connection. Local building permits apply. Pool villas in this corridor commonly sell in the $400K–$700K range once built.
faqPage.cat2a3: Partial sale starts from 1 rai. The full plot is 4 Rai 2 Ngan (7,200 m²). Contact the agent for current availability and pricing.
faqPage.cat2q4: How long does the transfer take?
faqPage.cat2a4: A straightforward Thai-to-Thai freehold transfer can complete in days once documents are ready. A registered foreign lease typically takes longer — often 2–6 weeks depending on lawyer preparation, translations and Land Office appointments.
faqPage.cat3q1: Is there a mortgage available for foreigners?
faqPage.cat3a1: Thai bank mortgages for foreign land buyers are generally not available in the same way as for Thai nationals. Most foreign buyers purchase with cash or home-country financing. Discuss options with your lawyer and bank.
faqPage.cat3q2: Why is it below market price?
faqPage.cat3a2: The asking price sits below several comparable Soi 112 listings of similar size and title. Motivated seller, large flat Chanote plot — see the comparable plots page for side-by-side numbers.
faqPage.cat3a3: The listing is represented by an independent local agent (LINE: 065-901-2984). Always use your own independent lawyer for due diligence — separate from the selling side.
faqPage.cat4a1: In 2026 Thailand is actively prosecuting illegal nominee land structures — Thai names holding land for foreigners. This plot uses a clean Chanote title with a Thai seller and no nominee setup. Read the full crackdown guide before any purchase.
faqPage.cat4q2: Is Hua Hin safe for foreigners?
faqPage.cat4a2: Hua Hin is a royal resort town with a long-standing international community, strong infrastructure and a calmer profile than mass party destinations. English is widely spoken at hospitals, malls and services. As anywhere, use normal personal security sense.

nomineeCrackdown.banner: ⚠️ Active enforcement — Thailand is prosecuting nominee land structures in 2026
nomineeCrackdown.heroLabel: Foreign buyer guide · Last updated July 2026
nomineeCrackdown.heroTitle: Thailand's Nominee Land Crackdown — The Complete Guide for Foreign Buyers
nomineeCrackdown.heroSub: 852 companies prosecuted. THB 15.1 billion in damages identified. AI scanning 50,000+ records. Here is everything you need to know — and what to do.
nomineeCrackdown.t1Body: Thailand's Department of Business Development activates the Intelligence Business Analytic System — an AI platform cross-referencing corporate registry, tax data, and land records in real time. 40,000–50,000 company and land transaction records scanned.
nomineeCrackdown.t2Body: New mandatory in-person verification requirements and investment confirmation letters take effect 1 April 2026. DBD and Land Department sign a formal data-exchange agreement. Central Investigation Bureau MOU signed — a flag in one agency triggers simultaneous investigation in others.
nomineeCrackdown.t3Body: Thailand's Cabinet (24 February 2026) acknowledges an inter-agency progress report and directs further legislative work — including a proposed amendment to Land Code Section 94 that would allow confiscation WITHOUT compensation.
nomineeCrackdown.t4Body: 100+ companies identified for deeper investigation in Pattaya alone. Rayong and Chonburi: 72 rai case under active legal proceedings.
nomineeCrackdown.t5Body: Pool villas and luxury hotel businesses inspected. Nominee network with combined value THB 200 million frozen and seized.
nomineeCrackdown.t6Body: 852 companies prosecuted. THB 15.1 billion in identified economic damages. Six priority sectors: tourism, land trade & real estate, e-commerce, agriculture, hotels & resorts, construction.
nomineeCrackdown.dangerP1: A Thai company where Thai shareholders are "nominees" — holding shares on paper while a foreigner controls and funds everything — is the core target of this crackdown.
nomineeCrackdown.dangerP2Mid: under the Land Code and the Foreign Business Act. What changed in 2025–2026 is not the law — it is
nomineeCrackdown.dangerP3After: include: multiple companies at the same address; shareholders whose financial status does not match share value; concentrated shareholding; and capital flows inconsistent with declared income.
nomineeCrackdown.section94BodyBefore: Not a forced sale at market value — but loss of the asset with
nomineeCrackdown.section94BodyAfter: . Not yet law, but under Cabinet review.
nomineeCrackdown.safeIntro: These structures are lawful when used correctly. Nominee companies are not among them.
nomineeCrackdown.row2Notes: Must be registered at Land Office
nomineeCrackdown.row4Notes: Must have real Thai business activity
nomineeCrackdown.plotTitle: Why this Soi 112 plot is different
nomineeCrackdown.plotLi1: — freehold, GPS-surveyed, verifiable at the Land Office.
nomineeCrackdown.plotLi2: — straightforward Land Office transfer, no company structure involved.
nomineeCrackdown.plotLi3After: as the legal structure for foreign buyers.
nomineeCrackdown.check3: Lease registration at Land Office — safe legal structure
nomineeCrackdown.ownTitle: Already own Thai land through a nominee company?
nomineeCrackdown.step1Title: Consult a Thai property lawyer immediately
nomineeCrackdown.step1Body: Do not wait. A licensed practitioner can assess your structure, exposure, and options under current rules.
nomineeCrackdown.step2Body: Under current law: typically a 180-day to 1-year disposal order — you keep proceeds from a forced sale. Act while this framework still applies.
nomineeCrackdown.step3Body: Proposed change (not yet law): confiscation without compensation. That would eliminate financial recovery if enacted.
nomineeCrackdown.step4Body: Show Thai shareholders are real investors with real financial capacity — not paper nominees. Substance is what IBAS and investigators look for.
nomineeCrackdown.step5Title: Consider restructuring to a registered lease
nomineeCrackdown.step5Body: Moving to a Land Office–registered lease before enforcement reaches you may be safer than waiting for an investigation flag.
nomineeCrackdown.ownWarning: The enforcement window is narrowing. Structures that depended on inconsistent local enforcement are no longer safe.
nomineeCrackdown.faq1Q: Does this affect my condo purchase?
nomineeCrackdown.faq1A: No. Condo freehold ownership within the 49% foreign quota is completely safe and not targeted.
nomineeCrackdown.faq2A: Yes — IF it is registered at the Land Office. An unregistered lease is not enforceable if disputed.
nomineeCrackdown.faq3A: Phuket, Koh Samui, Pattaya, Koh Pha-ngan are the current hot zones. The enforcement is national but resources are focused on tourist areas first.
nomineeCrackdown.faq4Q: Can I still buy through a Thai company?
nomineeCrackdown.faq4A: Only if the company has genuine Thai business activity and Thai shareholders who are real investors with real financial capacity. Nominee structures are illegal.
nomineeCrackdown.faq5A: The Intelligence Business Analytic System — an AI platform operated by Thailand's DBD since October 2025. It cross-references 40,000–50,000 company and land records to detect nominee patterns automatically.
nomineeCrackdown.faq6A: Not yet in the current enforcement wave, which focuses on Phuket, Samui and Pattaya. But enforcement is expanding nationally. Hua Hin buyers should not assume they are safe from future enforcement.
nomineeCrackdown.faq7Q: How do I verify a title deed?
nomineeCrackdown.faq7A: Visit the Land Office where the land is registered, or use data.hua-hin-land.com for a guide on what to check.
nomineeCrackdown.ctaTitle: This plot has a clean Chanote title and Thai seller — no nominee structure.
nomineeCrackdown.legalDisclaimer: This page provides general information based on publicly available sources as of July 2026. Nothing herein constitutes legal advice. Laws and enforcement priorities change — always consult a licensed Thai property lawyer before making any property decision.

townPage.title: Hua Hin town — 17 minutes.
townPage.subtitle: A real town. Not a tourist trap.
townPage.differentBody1: Hua Hin is not Pattaya. It is not Phuket. It has resisted the worst excesses of Thai beach tourism and retained a genuinely pleasant, mixed character — Thai families, European retirees, golfers, foodies, and a small but real arts community all coexist here.
townPage.differentBody2: The town centre has a proper night market, excellent seafood restaurants, international bars with live music, a Sunday walking street, and a railway station with direct trains to Bangkok. It functions as a real place, not a stage set.
townPage.nightlifeCaption: Restaurants & nightlife in Hua Hin town
townPage.nightlifeBody1: Live music every night. Jazz bars, Thai bands, international acts. A cosmopolitan restaurant scene with everything from street food to fine dining. Night markets on weekends. And a community of people who know how to enjoy an evening without it becoming a problem.
townPage.nightlifeBody2: Hua Hin's nightlife is relaxed, friendly and international — not raucous. The kind you can take your parents to, or enjoy with a partner who doesn't drink. The kind you actually want on your doorstep.

sonResortPage.heroAlt: The Son Resort private pool villa Pranburi
sonResortPage.title: The Son Resort — best rated resort in the area
sonResortPage.subtitle: Wang Pong, Pranburi · ⭐ 5.0 perfect score
sonResortPage.figCaption: The Son Resort · Wang Pong, Pranburi · 25 min from plot
sonResortPage.resortBody: Top-rated boutique resort in Wang Pong, Pranburi — a perfect 5.0 rating from 142 reviews. When a highly rated resort chooses to operate in this region, it validates the area's appeal for high-end tourism and lifestyle buyers.
sonResortPage.resortFeatures: Private pool villas · boutique resort · gateway to Khao Sam Roi Yot National Park · exceptional value
sonResortPage.buyersTitle: 🏡 What this means for land buyers
sonResortPage.buyersBody: Resort operators invest where guests want to stay. A perfect-rated resort nearby is a market signal — the area attracts visitors willing to pay for quality, which supports villa rental yields and resale values for residential development on adjacent land.

pranburiPage.title: Pranburi — a second coastline, 20 minutes away
pranburiPage.subtitle: Quieter beaches and a growing wellness community south of Hua Hin
pranburiPage.beachBody: Pranburi Beach: quieter, less commercial alternative to Hua Hin beach. A second coastline within easy reach — ideal for weekend escapes, morning swims, and a change of scenery without a long drive.
pranburiPage.beachFeatures: Quiet local beach · mangrove forest boardwalk · river boat tours · fishing village · 20 min from plot
pranburiPage.nature1: • Pranburi Forest Park and mangrove walks — boardwalks through coastal forest
pranburiPage.nature2: • Growing expat and wellness community — boutique resorts and eco-lodges
pranburiPage.nature3: • Pak Nam Pran fishing village — authentic local atmosphere and fresh seafood
pranburiPage.nature4: • Ko Sai and surrounding coastal area — closer than most expect from satellite view

bangkokPage.title: Bangkok — 200 km, 2.5 hours.
bangkokPage.subtitle: World-class city within easy weekend reach
bangkokPage.connectionBody: Hua Hin sits 200 km south of Bangkok on the Gulf coast — close enough to drive for a weekend, far enough to feel like a different world. Many Bangkok residents use Hua Hin as their weekend retreat. Many Hua Hin residents use Bangkok for specialist medical care, international flights, shopping and business.
bangkokPage.flightsBody1: Suvarnabhumi Airport (BKK) is one of Asia's major hubs — direct flights to Amsterdam, Frankfurt, London, Zurich, Dubai, Singapore, Tokyo and hundreds of other destinations. Flying in and out of Bangkok is straightforward and well-connected.
bangkokPage.flightsBody2: Hua Hin also has its own small airport (HHQ) with domestic connections to Bangkok (Don Mueang) — 50 minutes, several times daily.

chumphonPage.title: Chumphon — 230 km south.
chumphonPage.subtitle: Gateway to the islands, end of the Gulf coast
chumphonPage.whyBody: Chumphon is the jumping-off point for Koh Tao — Thailand's world-famous diving island. It's also where the Gulf coast transitions to a more tropical, island-dotted landscape. A 3-hour drive from Soi 112 opens up a completely different world.
chumphonPage.item1Note: 2-hour ferry from Chumphon · world's best dive sites for beginners
chumphonPage.item2Note: accessible via Chumphon or Surat Thani (another hour south)
chumphonPage.item3Note: Full Moon Party island, also via Surat Thani
chumphonPage.item4Note: authentic Thai provincial city, minimal tourism
chumphonPage.biggerBody1: Hua Hin's location on the upper Gulf coast means you're within a day's drive of almost everything Thailand has to offer. North to Bangkok and beyond. South to the islands and eventually Malaysia. West across the peninsula to Kanchanaburi and the Andaman coast.
chumphonPage.biggerBody2: This is a genuinely central location for exploring Thailand — not a remote destination, not an urban centre. The best of both.

accessPage.title: Access — the best of both worlds.
accessPage.subtitle: Close enough to everything. Far enough from the noise.
accessPage.roadBody1: The plot sits directly on a concrete road — no dirt tracks, no seasonal access issues, no getting stuck in the rainy season. You can drive a truck to the plot on day one. Construction vehicles, delivery lorries, your own car — all fine year-round.
accessPage.roadBody2: This matters more than it sounds. Many attractive plots in Thailand are accessed via dirt or laterite tracks that become impassable in heavy rain. Not here.
accessPage.distancesTitle: 📍 The sweet spot — Soi 112, Thap Tai
accessPage.distancesBody: Soi 112 sits at the edge of Hua Hin's urban spread — developed enough to have infrastructure, quiet enough to feel like the countryside. It's a genuinely pleasant place to live, not just to visit.
accessPage.quietBody: Soi 112 is a residential corridor, not a busy through-road. Traffic is light — mostly residents and the occasional delivery. There are no factories, no night markets on the doorstep, no airport flight paths.
accessPage.quiet1: No road noise from the main highway — Soi 112 is a side road
accessPage.quiet2: No industrial neighbours — purely residential and agricultural area
accessPage.quiet3: National park behind the plot — silence in that direction guaranteed
accessPage.quiet4: Low traffic density — this is a neighbourhood road, not a commuter route
accessPage.quietNote: The loudest thing you'll hear most mornings is birdsong from the national park.
accessPage.ctaTitle: Questions about access to the plot?
accessPage.ctaBody: Concrete road, year-round access, Soi 112 — message us on LINE.

terrainPage.title: The terrain — flat, buildable, ready.
terrainPage.subtitle: What the land looks like and what it means for your build
terrainPage.conditionBody1: The plot is largely flat with natural grass and low vegetation. The terrain is level across most of the surface — no significant slopes, no rocky outcrops, no swampy areas. This is genuinely good building land.
terrainPage.conditionBody2: Like most plots in Hua Hin, some raising of the ground level (ophogen) is advisable before construction — primarily to bring the foundation above the seasonal high water table and to meet standard building codes. This is completely normal and expected for any new villa build in the area.
terrainPage.raiseTitle: 📊 Raising the ground level — what to expect
terrainPage.whyBody: Raising the plot by 50–80 cm ensures drainage, prevents flooding risk, and meets standard Thai building requirements for residential construction.
terrainPage.phaseBody: You don't need to raise the entire 7,200 m² at once. Most developers start with the area they're building on first, then raise the remainder as they develop further plots — significantly reducing upfront costs.
terrainPage.costBody: Approx. ฿120–180 per m³ of fill material. For a standard villa footprint of 400 m² raised 60 cm, budget around ฿30,000–50,000. For the full plot, total raising costs would be in the region of ฿150,000–300,000.
terrainPage.opportunityBody: Because you control the full 4 Rai 2 Ngan, you can design the raising to create natural privacy levels between plots, integrate drainage intelligently, and add topographical interest to what would otherwise be a flat garden.
terrainPage.advantagesTitle: ✅ Why flat land is actually an advantage
terrainPage.adv1Body: no expensive retaining walls or hillside engineering
terrainPage.adv2Body: standard pile foundation, no special requirements
terrainPage.adv3Body: build in any orientation, pools anywhere
terrainPage.adv4Body: no awkward slopes to work around
terrainPage.ctaTitle: Ready to build on flat Chanote land?
terrainPage.ctaBody: 4 Rai 2 Ngan on Soi 112 — message us on LINE.

viewPage.title: The view that changes everything.
viewPage.subtitle: Panoramic mountain views — morning, noon and golden hour
viewPage.viewTitle: 🏔️ What you see from the plot
viewPage.viewBody1: The Thap Tai mountains rise directly behind the plot — a lush, green limestone ridge that stays cool and forested year-round. There are no buildings between the plot and the mountains. The view is unobstructed and, given the protected forest status, will remain so.
viewPage.viewBody2: In the morning, mist sometimes rolls through the valleys between the peaks — a sight that people travel hours to see. At golden hour, the mountains turn amber and the sky behind them shifts through every shade of orange, pink and purple. This is the view from your future terrace.
viewPage.morningBody1: You wake up, brew a proper coffee or steep a matcha. You step out onto your terrace — the air is still cool, the birds are starting. The mountains are right there, close enough to feel their presence, far enough to give you space and silence.
viewPage.morningBody2: In the evening, a glass of wine. The mountains catch the last light. The temperature drops just enough to be perfect. This is not a holiday postcard — this is a Tuesday.
viewPage.morningBody3: This is what the view does to a life. It slows it down. In the best possible way.
viewPage.protect1: The mountains behind the plot are protected national park — no development allowed
viewPage.protect2: Thap Tai National Park designation ensures permanent green backdrop
viewPage.protect3: No high-rise development possible in this zone — low density area
viewPage.protect4: The view you see today is the view you will see in 20 years
viewPage.ctaTitle: Build with this view every day.
viewPage.ctaBody: 4 Rai 2 Ngan with mountain views on Soi 112 — message us on LINE.

waterPage.title: Water — sorted from day one.
waterPage.subtitle: Municipal connection in place, with well as backup option
waterPage.waterBody1: The plot already has a municipal water connection in place — the pipe is there, the connection is made. This saves significant time and cost compared to plots where water infrastructure still needs to be arranged.
waterPage.waterBody2: Once you begin construction, connecting your building to the existing supply is straightforward and handled by your contractor as part of standard site work.
waterPage.backupTitle: 🪣 Drilling a well — a smart backup
waterPage.backupBody: Many villa owners in Hua Hin drill a private well as a secondary water source — particularly useful for garden irrigation, pool top-up, and as backup during any temporary supply interruptions.
waterPage.powerBody: Electricity is not yet connected to the plot but is available from the PEA (Provincial Electricity Authority) infrastructure along Soi 112. Connection is a standard process handled during construction.
waterPage.ctaBody: Municipal water in place on Soi 112 — message us on LINE.

chanotePage.title: Chanote — the gold standard.
chanotePage.subtitle: Understanding Thai land title deeds
chanotePage.chanoteName: Chanote (โฉนดที่ดิน · NS.4J)
chanotePage.chanoteBody: Full ownership title. GPS-surveyed boundaries registered at the Land Department. Can be sold, mortgaged, leased or subdivided freely. This is what this plot has.
chanotePage.ns3gBody: Strong title but boundaries not GPS-confirmed. Can be upgraded to Chanote. Transactions possible but require more due diligence.
chanotePage.ns3Body: Possession right only. Boundaries may be disputed. Transactions require 30-day public notice period. Not recommended for investment.
chanotePage.spkName: Sor Por Kor / Por Bor Tor 5
chanotePage.spkBody: Agricultural use permits only. Cannot be sold or transferred. No investment value.
chanotePage.why4Body: split into smaller plots if desired
chanotePage.why5Body: can be used as collateral for a construction loan
chanotePage.foreignIntro: Thai law restricts direct foreign land ownership. However, several legal structures allow foreigners to control and benefit from land:
chanotePage.foreign1Body: land held by a Thai limited company with foreign director
chanotePage.foreign3Body: land in Thai name with usufruct rights
chanotePage.foreign4Body: certain investments qualify for direct ownership
chanotePage.foreignNote: Always seek independent legal advice from a qualified Thai property lawyer before purchasing.
chanotePage.ctaBody: Verify the title deed and ask us anything — message on LINE.

beachPage.title: Hua Hin Beach — 12 minutes.
beachPage.subtitle: Thailand's original royal seaside resort
beachPage.aboutBody1: Hua Hin has been Thailand's royal beach resort for over a century — King Rama VII built the royal palace Klai Kangwon here in 1926, and the Thai royal family continues to use it today. This history gives Hua Hin a dignity and stability that many other Thai beach towns lack.
beachPage.aboutBody2: The beach itself stretches 6 kilometres — long, relatively wide, and lined with casuarina trees. The water is calm for most of the year. It's not the turquoise Andaman — but it's real, uncrowded by Thai resort standards, and genuinely beautiful at sunrise and sunset.
beachPage.beach1Note: 12 min · 6km, royal resort town, restaurants, horse riding
beachPage.beach2Note: 15 min · quieter, temple on the hill, local atmosphere
beachPage.beach3Note: 30 min · secluded, boutique resorts, very quiet
beachPage.beach4Note: 60 min · pristine, national park, almost no tourists

hospitalPage.title: Bangkok Hospital — 15 minutes.
hospitalPage.subtitle: International standard healthcare at your doorstep
hospitalPage.bangkokBody: Bangkok Hospital is part of Thailand's premier private hospital network — the same group that operates hospitals used by Thai royalty, government officials and international business executives. The Hua Hin branch is a full-service facility with specialist departments, 24/7 emergency care, and English-speaking staff throughout.
hospitalPage.other2Note: 17 min · Public hospital, very low cost · +66 32 532 395
hospitalPage.other3Note: world-renowned wellness hospital on the beach

meditationPage.heroAlt: Buddha-Metta Meditation Center Hua Hin Thap Tai
meditationPage.title: Wellness & meditation — a rare neighbour
meditationPage.subtitle: Buddha-Metta Meditation Practice Center
meditationPage.centerBody: Internationally known meditation and retreat center, minutes from the plot. A landmark that signals the character of this area: quiet, contemplative, and deliberately removed from the commercial bustle of central Hua Hin.
meditationPage.centerFeatures: Branch of Khao Sanam Chai Temple · monks follow strict Vinaya precepts · Dhamma teachings in Pali and Thai · open to respectful visitors
meditationPage.why1: Appeals to wellness-focused buyers and digital nomads seeking calm surroundings
meditationPage.why2: Quiet, low-traffic surroundings enhance the meditation atmosphere of the entire area
meditationPage.why3: Confirms Soi 112 as a lifestyle corridor, not just a development zone
meditationPage.why4: Neighbouring a retreat center typically means protected quiet — no noisy commercial neighbours

sevenElevenPage.title: 7-Eleven — 4 minutes away.
sevenElevenPage.subtitle: Why this matters more than you think
sevenElevenPage.aboutTitle: 🏪 7-Eleven in Thailand — not just a convenience store
sevenElevenPage.aboutBody1: In Thailand, 7-Eleven is infrastructure. With over 14,000 locations across the country, it is where Thais and expats alike pay bills, top up phone credit, pick up freshly made food, buy medicine, withdraw cash, send parcels, and much more.
sevenElevenPage.aboutBody2: Having one 4 minutes from your door means never running out of anything. Hot coffee at 6am. Cold beer at 10pm. Stamps, gas, snacks, ice, painkillers — all covered. It sounds trivial until you live somewhere without one.
sevenElevenPage.servicesTitle: 🧾 What you can do at 7-Eleven Thailand
sevenElevenPage.nearbyBody: The 7-Eleven on Soi 112 sits at a local junction that has become a small neighbourhood hub — with local food stalls, a pharmacy and petrol station within walking distance. This is the daily heartbeat of the area.

nationalParkPage.title: National Park — on your doorstep.
nationalParkPage.subtitle: Wild Thailand, five minutes from your front gate
nationalParkPage.thapBody: The mountains you see from the plot are Thap Tai National Park. Protected forest, limestone peaks, waterfalls and caves — all within minutes of the plot. This is not a managed tourist attraction. It's wild forest that has been protected for decades.
nationalParkPage.feature1: Hiking trails from easy to challenging
nationalParkPage.feature6: No entrance fee for the Thap Tai section
nationalParkPage.park1Note: 85 km · UNESCO World Heritage · largest in Thailand · exceptional birdwatching
nationalParkPage.park2Note: 60 km · limestone karsts, mangroves, Phraya Nakhon Cave with royal pavilion
nationalParkPage.park3Note: 85 km · wild elephant sightings with 95% daily probability — a genuinely astonishing experience
nationalParkPage.whyTitle: 🌅 Why having nature next door matters
nationalParkPage.whyBody: Studies consistently show that proximity to green space improves wellbeing, reduces stress and extends life expectancy. You don't need to go to the park — the park comes to you. Birds from the forest visit gardens on Soi 112. The air is noticeably cleaner. The silence at night is real.
nationalParkPage.whyItalic: And because it's protected national park, this will never change. No development. No noise. Just mountain and forest, forever.

bluportPage.title: Bluport Mall — 10 minutes.
bluportPage.subtitle: Hua Hin's premier shopping and lifestyle destination
bluportPage.insideBody: Bluport Hua Hin Resort Mall is the most modern shopping centre in the region — opened in 2016 with international brands, restaurants, a cinema, supermarket and entertainment facilities. It's where Hua Hin residents do their serious shopping.
bluportPage.item1Note: full international supermarket with imported goods
bluportPage.item2Note: modern cinema with English language films
bluportPage.marketBody: Adjacent to Bluport is Market Village — an older mall with more everyday stores, a large Tesco Lotus supermarket, hardware shops, phone repair, and dozens of local restaurants. Together, these two malls cover virtually every shopping need.

comparablePlotsPage.title: How does our plot compare?
comparablePlotsPage.subtitle: We benchmarked every comparable plot currently listed on Soi 112 and surrounding area.
comparablePlotsPage.conclusion: Our plot is Chanote titled, flat, water connected, and priced below comparable Soi 112 listings — in one piece at 4 rai 2 ngan, a rare size in this corridor.
comparablePlotsPage.source: Data based on active listings from Hipflat, FazWaz, Harmony Real Estate and Dot Property — July 2025

partialSalePage.title: Buying part of the plot — what you need to know
partialSalePage.subtitle: Partial sale is possible from 1 rai. Here is what the process looks like and what costs to expect as a buyer.
howItems: The plot is 4 rai 2 ngan with a single Chanote title
On partial sale, the plot is officially subdivided at the Thai Land Department
Each buyer receives their own Chanote title in their name — the strongest form of land ownership in Thailand
Flat terrain, water connection in place, access via Soi 112

steps.title: Agreement on size and boundaries
steps.desc: Buyer and seller agree on the exact size and boundaries of the portion being sold.

steps.title: Official land surveyor
steps.desc: A licensed surveyor measures and maps the new plot boundaries — approximately 2–4 weeks.

steps.title: Subdivision application at Land Department
steps.desc: Formal application to split the Chanote title — approximately 4–8 weeks.

steps.title: New Chanote titles issued
steps.desc: Each buyer receives their own separate Chanote title deed in their name.

partialSalePage.timeline: Total timeline: approximately 2–3 months from agreement to new title deeds.

costs.amount: 2% of official appraised value — often split 50/50 with seller, so buyer pays ~1%

partialSalePage.note: Note: Stamp duty (0.5%), Specific Business Tax (3.3%) and Withholding Tax are costs for the seller, not the buyer. The exact split of the transfer fee is negotiable and will be agreed in the Sale & Purchase Agreement. All amounts are indicative — consult a Thai property lawyer before committing.

liveAndProfitPage.title: Live & profit — build your villa, sell the rest
liveAndProfitPage.concept: Buy 4 rai 2 ngan in one piece. Build your own villa on 1 rai. Subdivide the remaining land into 3–4 plots of approximately 1 rai each and sell them individually — keeping your home while recovering most or all of your land investment.

calcRows.label: Pool villas on Soi 112 start at

liveAndProfitPage.calcNote: Illustrative example — actual returns depend on build costs, subdivision permits and market conditions.

boutiquePage.title: Boutique development — 4 to 5 luxury pool villas
boutiquePage.concept: Develop the entire 4 rai 2 ngan plot as a small-scale luxury villa project — 4 to 5 pool villas designed for sale or holiday rental. Soi 112's prestige corridor supports premium pricing and strong demand from both Thai and international buyers.
marketItems: Rental yields in Hua Hin average 6–10% per year
Pool villas on Soi 112 start at ฿15M+
Strong demand from retirees, second-home buyers and holiday rental market
Chanote title allows subdivision and individual sale per villa plot
boutiquePage.calcNote: Illustrative example — consult a local developer and lawyer for accurate feasibility studies.

privateEstatePage.title: Private estate — your own tropical compound
privateEstatePage.concept: Keep the full 4 rai 2 ngan plot for yourself. Build a main villa, guest house, large swimming pool and tropical gardens — all on one private compound with no neighbours overlooking your land. This is the option for buyers who want space, privacy and a legacy property rather than a development project.
privateEstatePage.whyTitle: ✅ Why this plot suits a private estate
whyItems: Unobstructed mountain views — protected national park backdrop
4 rai 2 ngan of privacy — rare size for a single-owner compound on Soi 112
Soi 112 luxury corridor — Botanica, La Felice, Mali Prestige as neighbours
Flat terrain — flexible layout for main house, pool and gardens
Water connected — infrastructure ready for construction
privateEstatePage.statProtectedLabel: Unobstructed mountain views — protected national park backdrop
privateEstatePage.statFlatLabel: Flat terrain — flexible layout for main house, pool and gardens

sections.title: 6. Thai Property Law & Foreign Ownership
strongParagraphs: This section is particularly important for non-Thai nationals.
paragraphs: Under the Land Code Act B.E. 2497 (พระราชบัญญัติที่ดิน พ.ศ. ๒๔๙๗), foreign nationals are generally prohibited from owning land freehold in Thailand. This restriction applies regardless of nationality, residence status, or visa type.
Potential foreign buyers must seek independent legal advice from a licensed Thai lawyer (ทนายความ) regarding legally available ownership structures, which may include but are not limited to:

The website owner makes no representation that any particular ownership structure is legally available, appropriate, or advisable for any particular buyer. All ownership structures carry legal risk and must be assessed by a qualified Thai lawyer.
The Thai Land Department has full discretion over property transfers. Approval of any transaction is not guaranteed.
This website does not constitute legal advice on foreign ownership and nothing herein should be construed as such.

items: Long-term leasehold under Section 540 of the Thai Civil and Commercial Code (maximum 30 years per registration, potentially renewable by agreement)

Ownership through a Thai limited company, subject to the Foreign Business Act B.E. 2542 (พระราชบัญญัติการประกอบธุรกิจของคนต่างด้าว พ.ศ. ๒๕๔๒) and requirements that Thai nationals hold a genuine majority shareholding
Usufruct rights under Sections 1417–1428 of the Thai Civil and Commercial Code

Inheritance rights, subject to applicable Thai succession law
